% Methodology: ΔY budget measured w.r.t. ORIGINAL clean reps' Y (matches Tab.~\ref{tab:main_results_unified}).
% A row's "alone" cell shows |S - S_floor| at the baseline endpoint ONLY if
% that endpoint stays within the budget of clean Y; otherwise the baseline
% spent its budget overshooting and is excluded from "alone reach".
\begin{table}[t]
\centering
\caption{\textbf{TanGCE complements every Phase-1 baseline.} Mean $|S{-}S_{\text{floor}}|$ in pp at the trajectory step closest to chance, with $\Delta Y$ measured against the original clean reps (same budget convention as Tab.~\ref{tab:main_results_unified}). For each baseline B, the ``alone'' column reports B's endpoint leakage on settings where B kept $Y$ within the budget; the ``+TanGCE'' column reports the best post-TanGCE step on the same settings. Reach $\!=\!$ (settings in budget)$/$(settings with data). \TanGCE{}-only and \TanGCEpp{} are reference rows for context (TanGCE on clean reps; full SOTA pipeline). $^\dagger${}\textbf{Obliviator surgicality warning:} Obliviator overshoots the $\Delta Y$ budget on \emph{every} sycophancy and gender setting ($26/26$); the only settings on which it lands in budget are the $13$ safety settings, where the safety task accuracy floor happens to coincide with Obliviator's encoder-collapse Y. The $0.0$pp leakage on those $13$ settings reflects this collapse, not a genuine surgical erasure --- it is an artefact of Obliviator destroying control accuracy until $Y$ matches the panel's $Y_{\text{floor}}$.}
\label{tab:tangce_on_baseline}
